latest.title: Appointment of Chief Executive Officer Designate
latest.content:
16 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 6581A
IG Design Group PLC
16 April 2026
16 April 2026
IG Design Group plc
(the "Company", the "Group" or "Design Group")
Appointment of
Chief Executive Officer Designate
IG Design Group plc, a leading designer, innovator and manufacturer across various celebration and creative categories, is pleased to announce the appointment of Gerald Kuehr as Chief Executive Officer Designate, with effect from 1 May 2026. On 1 July 2026, Gerald will assume the role as Chief Executive Officer and Stewart Gilliland, who has led the Group as Interim Executive Chair, will revert to his role as Non-Executive Chair upon Gerald's appointment.
Gerald has been working with Design Group in an advisory capacity to DG Europe since January 2026, during which time he has gained a deep understanding of the Group's strategic priorities, operations and customers, many of whom he has partnered with before. Additionally, he has helped segments of the Group to refocus its energy on its core business whilst supporting the team in developing plans to secure new business. His transition into the Chief Executive Officer role reflects the Board's confidence in his leadership as the Group continues to strengthen and follows the
defined pathway to further improvement in profitability and long-term value creation
.
Gerald brings over 30 years of international leadership experience in blue chip consumer businesses. Most recently, he served as Chief Executive Officer of Partner in Pet Food (PPF) from 2020 to 2025, where he repositioned the business from an Eastern European, volume-driven private-label player into a pan-European category leader operating across ten markets with 3,000 employees. During his tenure, he also completed five acquisitions.
Prior to this, Gerald held senior global leadership roles at Unilever, most recently as Global Chief Customer Officer, where he was a member of the Global Leadership Team with responsibility for sales strategy, commercial excellence and customer development across 30 markets. He was previously an Associate Partner at Bain & Company and spent 14 years at Procter & Gamble, including as General Manager for Germany and Austria.
Gerald Christoph Kuehr, aged 58, has held the following directorships/partnerships in the past five years:
Current Directorships/Partnerships
Former Directorships/Partnerships (past five years)
-
Woof Investments SARL
Artemis Acquisitions Topco Limited
Partner in Pet Food Sweden AB
Doggy AB
Partner in Pet Food France SAS
PPF HoldCo B.V.
Partner in Pet Food Group B.V.
Partner in Pet Food NL B.V.
Partner in Pet Food Poland
Partner in Pet Food Hungaria Kft
Kollmax Kft
Mr Kuehr is not currently a director of any companies.
Save as set out above, there is no further information to be disclosed in relation to Mr Kuehr's appointment pursuant to AIM Rule 17 or paragraph (g) of Schedule Two of the AIM Rules for Companies.
Stewart Gilliland, Chair of IG Design Group plc, commented:
"Having worked with Gerald over the past months, I have seen first-hand the calibre of his leadership and am therefore delighted that he is joining the Group as its Chief Executive Officer. The thorough search process, conducted with external advisors, identified a number of strong candidates, but Gerald's energy and passion set him apart. This, alongside his
exceptionally strong retail relationships across Europe, his proven track record of commercial improvement and delivering international growth, particularly within complex multi-market environments, makes him exceptionally well placed to lead the Group through its next phase. On behalf of the Board, I warmly welcome him into the role and look forward to supporting him in the period ahead."
Gerald Kuehr, incoming Chief Executive Officer, added:
"It is a privilege to be stepping into the Chief Executive Officer role at IG Design Group. My time working with DG Europe has given me an appreciation for the strength of the Group's portfolio, the talent of its people and the opportunity ahead. I look forward to working with the Board and to partnering with our customers, with whom we have excellent relationships with, as we unlock the Group's full potential."
For further information, please contact:
IG Design Group plc
Tel: +44 (0)1525 887310
Stewart Gilliland, Interim Executive Chair
Rohan Cummings, Chief Financial Officer
Canaccord Genuity Limited (Nomad and Broker)
Tel: +44 (0)20 7523 8000
Bobbie Hilliam
Elizabeth Halley-Stott
Alma Strategic Communications
Tel: +44 (0)20 3405 0205
Rebecca Sanders-Hewett
designgroup@almastrategic.com
Sam Modlin
Will Merison
Notes to Editors:
IG Design Group plc is one of the world's leading designers, innovators and manufacturers of gift packaging, greeting cards, stationery, creative play products and related items. The Group designs, sources and manufactures a broad portfolio of products across multiple categories and occasions, encompassing every day, seasonal and celebration ranges.
The Group operates across key international markets, with a strong presence in the United Kingdom, Europe and Australia. Its products are sold to a diverse customer base including major retailers and supermarkets such as Tesco, Costco and Aldi, as well as discounters, online platforms and independent stores. The Group's heritage brand, Tom Smith, holds a Royal Warrant for the supply of Christmas crackers and Christmas wrapping paper to the Royal Family, a distinction it has held continuously since 1906.
Across IG Design Group's global teams, it manages a portfolio of more than 22,000 SKUs, supplying over 550 million units annually to customers in approximately 70 countries. The Group operates a vertically integrated model, combining in
‑
house design expertise with global sourcing, manufacturing, distribution and fulfilment capabilities. Its manufacturing footprint includes three facilities located in Wales, the Netherlands and Poland. This integrated platform enables the Group to deliver innovative, responsibly sourced products at scale, while maintaining cost efficiency, speed to market and flexibility.
IG Design Group plc is listed on the AIM market of the London Stock Exchange.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.title: Completion of surplus UK warehouse sale
latest.content:
25 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 9578X
IG Design Group PLC
25 March 2026
25 March 2026
IG Design Group plc
(the "Company", the "Group" or "Design Group")
Completion of surplus UK warehouse sale
IG Design Group plc, a leading designer, innovator and manufacturer across various celebration and creative categories, announces that its subsidiary, IG Design Group UK Limited, has completed the sale of a surplus warehouse at Hirwaun Industrial Estate, Aberdare to the Welsh Government for c.£3.1 million. As previously indicated in the February 2026 trading update, this disposal forms part of the Group's efforts to strengthen its balance sheet. The carrying value of the site was £0.1 million and the sale will realise a profit of £2.9 million (after £0.1m of selling costs and taxes).
​For further information, please contact:
IG Design Group plc
Tel: +44 (0)1525 887310
Stewart Gilliland, Interim Executive Chair
Rohan Cummings, Chief Financial Officer
Canaccord Genuity Limited (Nomad and Broker)
Tel: +44 (0)20 7523 8000
Bobbie Hilliam
Elizabeth Halley-Stott
Alma Strategic Communications
Tel: +44 (0)20 3405 0205

designgroup@almastrategic.com

company_30s.what_they_do: IGR Ltd. is a specialist in the design, manufacture and distribution of decorative and functional packaging solutions for the food, beverage and retail sectors. Their products include custom‑printed paper cartons, folding cartons, trays and protective packaging used by supermarkets, restaurants and beverage distributors.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company serves a very large and stable market – the global packaging industry is worth over $300 billion and is expected to grow at 3–4% annually. IGR’s niche focus on premium, custom‑printed packaging gives it a competitive edge: clients pay a premium for branding, sustainability and quick turnaround, which helps the firm maintain healthy margins even when commodity costs rise.
company_30s.current_state: IGR has recently completed a strategic review of its UK warehouse portfolio, selling excess space and tightening its capital structure. The management team is in the process of replacing the CEO and has announced a capital‑reduction plan to improve the balance sheet. The stock is trading at roughly 0.00p, a fraction of its all‑time high, and is currently in a deep, almost 90% decline from that peak.

rns_narrative: The company is restructuring its leadership and capital base while trimming real‑estate overhead. The CEO appointment signals a new strategic direction, and the capital‑reduction plan is aimed at sharpening the balance sheet for future growth.

The warehouse sale and the trading update show that the business is actively cutting costs and improving cash flow, suggesting the company is moving from a defensive posture to a more aggressive execution mode.
These moves together point to a company that has survived a severe downturn and is now positioning itself to capture market share when the packaging market recovers.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – the chatter is almost entirely bullish, with very few counter‑voices.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls are praising the recent warehouse sale as a win for cash flow and the new CEO as a catalyst for turnaround. They see the capital‑reduction plan as a signal that the company will become more efficient and able to invest in higher‑margin products.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears warn that the company is still heavily reliant on a few large clients and that the recent leadership shuffle may not be enough to offset the long‑term decline in demand for traditional packaging. They also point out that the stock remains at a very low valuation, which could reflect deeper structural issues.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The sentiment has moved from a period of near‑silence to a modest but growing bullish tone, largely driven by the latest RNS updates. The community is still cautious, but the lack of negative chatter is a contrarian green light.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – there is no public search interest. This absence of noise means that a surprise catalyst could trigger a big move.

near_term_catalysts: The completion of the capital‑reduction plan, which is scheduled to finish in Q3, could free up equity and improve the balance sheet, making the company an attractive target for strategic investors.
A scheduled product launch of a new eco‑friendly packaging line in the next quarter could capture a growing segment of the market that is willing to pay a premium for sustainability.
If the company secures a multi‑year supply contract with a major retailer or beverage brand, the resulting revenue certainty would likely lift investor confidence.
expect_next.watch_for: Monitor the stock’s daily volume for a sudden spike – a 3‑to‑5‑fold increase would confirm renewed interest. Also keep an eye on the next earnings release; a surprise beat on revenue or EBITDA would validate the turnaround thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: IGR appears to be in the early stages of a capitulation cycle – the price has collapsed dramatically, but the company’s recent actions suggest the bottom is near. The APEX score indicates a tight compression, meaning that a single catalyst could push the price sharply higher.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is down over 90% from its peak – a classic deep‑crash scenario. Historically, such bottoms have returned 2–3× the drawdown once a catalyst triggers mean reversion. With a 90% decline, a 50% recovery to half the drawdown would bring the price back to 45% of the peak, which is a 2.2× upside. The new CEO and capital‑reduction plan provide tangible levers that could unlock that upside.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still faces a narrow client base and a high dependency on traditional packaging, which could limit growth if the market shifts. If the capital‑reduction plan fails to improve cash flow or the new product line is delayed, the stock could remain stuck in a low‑valuation range. Worst‑case, the price might stay below 0.01p, a 10× loss from the peak.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~2.5× (if the price climbs to the halfway point of the drawdown). Potential downside: up to 90% of the peak. The asymmetry is roughly 1:4 – you could lose almost all of what you invested for the chance to gain a 2.5× return.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a clear volume spike or a positive earnings surprise, buying at the support level around 0.00p. Use a 50% of the current price as a stop‑loss – if the price falls below that, the turnaround thesis is likely broken. Size the position so that a 50% move to the upside would cover the stop‑loss and yield a 2× return.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the next quarterly earnings. If revenue beats expectations by >10% or the company announces a new multi‑year contract, consider a small entry. A price rise of 20–30% on high volume would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy, is delisted, or if the new CEO resigns before Q4, the trade should be exited immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: IGR is a classic deep‑crash play – it’s 90% down, the market is silent, and the new CEO plus a capital‑reduction plan could trigger a 2.5× rebound, giving a highly asymmetric upside if the catalyst materialises.
